name: private-pypi
vendor: huntzhan (project later organized under the "pywharf" GitHub org)
cost: Free (MIT open source); no paid tiers or hosted service.
last_updated: 2022-02-27 (GitHub repo archived read-only). Code was effectively unmaintained well before that: the last pywharf release (0.2.3) shipped 2020-07-11, and the original private-pypi PyPI name last released 0.2.0 on 2020-03-25.
perf_notes: No published benchmarks. Python/Flask-based single-process server acting as an abstraction layer over a storage backend; serves a PEP 503 simple index synchronized from the backend on a configurable interval (default ~60s). Practical file-size ceilings come from the backend: GitHub backend ~2 GB/file (GitHub release-asset limit), filesystem backend default max 5 GB/file. Intended for small team/internal scale, not high throughput.
scale_notes: Single-instance server with no built-in HA, clustering, or multi-node coordination. Durable state lives entirely in the backend (GitHub release assets or local filesystem) plus a synced local index, so it is not designed for stateless horizontal scaling. Project is archived/unmaintained since Feb 2022; only GitHub and filesystem backends ever shipped despite a pluggable backend design.
summary: An archived, MIT-licensed self-hosted private PyPI server that sits as a thin PEP 503 + legacy-upload abstraction layer over flexible storage backends, notably using GitHub Releases (or a local filesystem) as the package store.
extras: Uses GitHub Releases as a first-class storage backend: packages are stored as release assets in a GitHub repo, authenticated with a personal access token (PAT)
Optional GitHub Pages static-index generation (github.gen_gh_pages) plus a GitHub Actions workflow to auto-update the remote index
Pluggable backend architecture intended to make adding new storage backends low-cost (only GitHub + local filesystem ever shipped)
Split remote/local index model: a standalone 'update_index' command writes the backend index and the server syncs a local copy on an interval (index_mtime endpoint)
Runtime reconfiguration via POST /initialize and a browser /login + /logout session flow
Per-package-repo read_secret/write_secret credentials, supplied as the HTTP Basic password (read vs write capability split)
Renamed from private-pypi to pywharf; repository archived (read-only) on 2022-02-27 and unmaintained
